description: Odette is Singapore's defining luxury dining room, pairing Julien Royer's precise French cuisine with immaculate hospitality inside the National Gallery. The experience feels polished, serene and globally fluent, making it a first-choice reservation for affluent international diners. It is the city's clearest blend of culinary prestige, design-conscious refinement and special-occasion authority.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; National Gallery setting; world-ranked French fine dining; exceptional hospitality.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; official website

description: Les Amis remains one of Asia's great formal dining institutions, with a hushed Scotts Road setting, deeply serious wine program and an enduring reputation for polished French haute cuisine. The atmosphere is elegant and moneyed without feeling dated, and the service is tailored to affluent locals, diplomats and luxury travelers. It is a cornerstone of Singapore's high-end dining market.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; grand French institution; elite wine cellar; strong affluent local following.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; Condé Nast Traveler; official website

description: Zén delivers one of the city's most immersive luxury experiences, unfolding across multiple floors of a heritage shophouse with meticulous Nordic-Japanese precision. The atmosphere is intimate, highly choreographed and unmistakably expensive, appealing strongly to globally seasoned fine-dining guests. It is among Singapore's most complete expressions of modern gastronomic luxury.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; multi-floor shophouse experience; immersive luxury format; Nordic-Japanese finesse.

description: Labyrinth is one of Singapore's most important chef-driven restaurants, transforming local flavors and references into a deeply personal luxury tasting menu. Its position inside the Esplanade complex gives it a strong cultural context, while the cooking keeps it firmly in the international upper tier. It is a natural choice for affluent diners seeking a distinctly Singaporean fine-dining identity.
highlights: World-ranked modern Singaporean tasting menu; cultural-district setting; chef-driven flagship.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; official website

description: Burnt Ends is one of Singapore's most relevant luxury alternatives to classic fine dining, combining Michelin-level cooking with a dynamic, design-conscious Dempsey setting. The open-fire format, strong wine and cocktail culture and affluent international crowd make it as socially compelling as it is culinarily serious. It is exactly the kind of place many Michelin diners book instead of a more formal tasting room.
highlights: Dempsey destination; wood-fired signature; world-ranked; affluent social energy.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; official website

description: Meta offers one of Singapore's smartest contemporary luxury experiences, blending Korean sensibility with French technique in a highly polished Mohamed Sultan Road setting. The room is urbane and elegant rather than theatrical, and the cooking is consistently expressive and precise. It works equally well for seasoned gourmands and affluent business diners looking for a current yet refined address.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; Korean-French fusion; elegant city setting; world-ranked reputation.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; official website

description: Seroja has quickly become one of Singapore's most compelling luxury reservations, presenting seafood-focused cuisine inspired by the Malay Archipelago in a refined, contemporary room. The dining experience feels delicate, personal and polished, with enough emotional identity to stand out in a crowded luxury market. It is especially strong for diners seeking something regionally rooted yet globally sophisticated.
highlights: One Michelin star; Malay Archipelago focus; world-ranked; refined contemporary atmosphere.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; Condé Nast Traveler; official website

description: Euphoria offers a richly flavored, highly polished contemporary European experience in one of the city's more intimate high-end dining rooms. The mood is calm and elegant, while the cooking is distinctive enough to attract serious gastronomic travelers. Its place on Asia's 50 Best list confirms it as one of Singapore's true upper-tier luxury tables.
highlights: One Michelin star; intimate luxury room; world-ranked contemporary European tasting menu.
sources: Michelin Guide; Asia's 50 Best Restaurants; official website

description: Cloudstreet combines technical ambition, expressive flavors and a dramatic, high-design setting that has made it one of the city's most desirable special-occasion tables. The room feels cosmopolitan and modern, with a strong sense of occasion that appeals to affluent locals and well-traveled visitors alike. It is a natural bridge between formal fine dining and social luxury.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; design-led dining room; highly sought-after tasting menu; strong social relevance.
sources: Michelin Guide; Tatler Asia; official website

description: JAAN remains one of Singapore's clearest luxury-hotel dining experiences, with sweeping skyline views from Swissotel The Stamford and a polished, highly legible style of British-influenced fine dining. The room is serene, elevated and ideal for both celebratory dinners and affluent business dining. Its combination of outlook, service and cuisine keeps it firmly in the luxury top tier.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; skyline views; luxury-hotel setting; polished British-inspired fine dining.
sources: Michelin Guide; Tatler Asia; official website

description: Saint Pierre offers one of the city's most graceful waterfront luxury experiences, with refined French cooking and calm panoramic views over Marina Bay. The service is polished and discreet, and the restaurant appeals strongly to affluent guests who want elegance without excess. It is particularly well suited to travelers seeking a classic special-occasion Singapore dinner.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; waterfront Marina Bay views; refined French fine dining; strong occasion appeal.

description: Shoukouwa remains one of Singapore's most exclusive sushi experiences, pairing exacting Edomae craftsmanship with an intimate, rarefied room at One Fullerton. The format is minimal and highly controlled, aimed at luxury diners who value precision and quiet exclusivity over spectacle. It is one of the city's benchmark omakase reservations.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; intimate omakase counter; One Fullerton address; highly exclusive format.

description: Waku Ghin continues to hold strong relevance in Singapore's luxury market through its Marina Bay Sands setting, chef pedigree and polished, internationally legible style. The experience is sleek, affluent and unmistakably hotel-luxury in tone, with strong appeal for global travelers and high-spend celebratory diners. It remains one of the city's most recognizable upscale Japanese-European tables.
highlights: Marina Bay Sands luxury dining; chef Tetsuya Wakuda pedigree; polished high-spend experience.

description: Summer Pavilion is one of Singapore's most elegant Cantonese luxury dining rooms, set inside The Ritz-Carlton, Millenia Singapore and defined by exacting service and a tranquil contemporary interior. It appeals strongly to affluent local families, hotel guests and business diners seeking familiar luxury codes executed at a very high level. It remains one of the city's key upscale Chinese restaurant reservations.
highlights: Luxury-hotel Cantonese dining; refined polished room; strong affluent local and business appeal.

description: Thevar brings Indian flavors into a sharply contemporary fine-dining format that feels stylish, urban and distinctly current. The atmosphere is polished but energetic, which broadens its appeal beyond traditional tasting-menu regulars to affluent diners looking for something more vivid and expressive. It is one of the city's most important chef-driven restaurants.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; chef-driven Indian fine dining; stylish contemporary room; strong luxury relevance.

description: Nouri has long held a distinctive place in Singapore's luxury dining scene, with a globally minded tasting menu built around its idea of cross-cultural food identity. The room is quiet, mature and polished, and the experience appeals strongly to diners who want thoughtful creativity over overt theatricality. It remains a strong alternative to more conventional European fine dining.
highlights: Chef-driven conceptual tasting menu; polished intimate room; globally minded luxury format.

description: Braci combines refined modern Italian cooking with one of the more appealing rooftop settings in the Boat Quay area, giving it strong relevance for affluent diners who value atmosphere as much as culinary credibility. The room is intimate and polished, while the outlook adds a social edge that more formal restaurants often lack. It is an especially persuasive luxury alternative for date nights and entertaining.
highlights: Michelin-starred modern Italian; rooftop setting; Boat Quay social appeal; polished intimate dining room.

description: Buona Terra offers old-world luxury appeal through an elegant landed-house setting, gracious service and sophisticated Italian cuisine. The atmosphere is softer and more residential than many downtown tasting rooms, making it particularly attractive to affluent diners seeking calm refinement. It remains one of Singapore's strongest classic luxury Italian addresses.
highlights: Elegant house setting; Michelin-starred Italian fine dining; calm refined luxury atmosphere.

description: Born stands out for its deeply considered storytelling, polished service and stylishly restrained setting on Neil Road. The cuisine draws from Chinese heritage and European technique in a way that feels emotionally resonant as well as technically exacting. It is especially relevant for luxury diners seeking a more contemporary and personal alternative to classic fine dining codes.
highlights: Michelin-starred; elegant heritage shophouse setting; thoughtful chef-driven tasting menu.

description: Pangium gives Singapore a rare heritage-luxury proposition, set within the Botanic Gardens and built around chef Malcolm Lee's refined exploration of Straits cuisine. The setting is lush, elegant and unusually transportive, offering affluent diners something more atmospheric than a standard urban dining room. It is one of the city's most compelling culturally rooted luxury experiences.
highlights: Botanic Gardens setting; heritage-led fine dining; highly atmospheric luxury experience.

description: Marguerite is one of the city's most quietly luxurious dining experiences, set inside the Flower Dome precinct at Gardens by the Bay and built around a polished contemporary tasting menu. The mood is serene, refined and highly suitable for affluent travelers looking for beauty and culinary credibility in equal measure. It is an elegant counterpoint to Singapore's more urban luxury restaurants.
highlights: Gardens by the Bay setting; polished contemporary tasting menu; serene design-led atmosphere.

description: Claudine belongs in Singapore's high-end consideration set because it delivers the kind of design-led, socially magnetic luxury experience many affluent diners will choose over a Michelin tasting menu. Set in a beautifully repurposed chapel at Dempsey, it combines French cuisine, strong cocktails and obvious visual drama. It is an especially strong non-Michelin option for fashionable dinners and entertaining.
highlights: Historic chapel setting; Dempsey glamour; strong social appeal; highly design-led French restaurant.
sources: Condé Nast Traveler; Tatler Asia; official website

description: COTE Singapore earns its place through social power as much as culinary seriousness, bringing a luxury Korean steakhouse format into the heart of Orchard's affluent hotel-and-shopping district. The mood is polished, lively and high-spend, with strong wine and cocktail appeal. It is exactly the type of non-Michelin restaurant a Michelin customer might realistically choose for energy, design and group appeal.
highlights: Luxury Korean steakhouse; affluent Orchard crowd; strong wine and cocktail program; high social energy.
sources: Tatler Asia; Time Out; official website

description: Shisen Hanten remains one of Singapore's most credible upscale Chinese hotel restaurants, combining a luxury Orchard setting with consistently polished Sichuan-inflected cuisine. The room is comfortable and affluent rather than fashion-forward, making it especially suitable for family celebrations, business lunches and hotel dining. It belongs in the luxury set because of its enduring market relevance and service quality.
highlights: Luxury-hotel Chinese restaurant; polished Sichuan-influenced menu; strong business and family appeal.

description: Sushi Sakuta is one of the city's most exclusive recent luxury reservations, bringing a highly disciplined omakase experience to Dempsey in a format that feels intimate, expensive and quietly elite. The restaurant appeals strongly to affluent sushi purists who value precision, rarity and low-key exclusivity. It is a strong addition to Singapore's upper-tier Japanese dining landscape.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; intimate omakase; Dempsey exclusivity; highly disciplined luxury sushi format.
